dmMessages.text:
I'm summer reaching out on behalf of DINQ, which is a smart, credible profile for researchers, engineers, and founders — a place that brings together your GitHub, papers, projects, skills, and ideas into one clean, trusted identity. Across LinkedIn, GitHub, arXiv and Twitter, your work is real but scattered. DINQ solves this by creating a unified profile based on verifiable data — publications, repositories, contributions, and links that come directly from your own sources. What makes it different: - Credible identity: your GitHub, papers, projects and talks are shown with verifiable links - Low-maintenance: keeps your GitHub / publications up to date without extra manual work - Lightweight portfolio: a clean page you can share for applications, hiring, or collaborations - Social by design: makes it easier to find like-minded researchers, trusted builders, and potential co-founders Dinq is planing to partner with a small group of creators to share first impressions while its wider release on December 10th. If you feel it like the right fit, pls share your rate and december slot with us! Summer

Hi Robert!

My name is Jo. I saw that you retweeted something fun about AGIBOT packing itself into a box, and I thought there is potential collaboration there. I work with multiple robotics brands (manufacturers, tech companies, etc.) on ongoing campaigns — product launches, event recaps, industry announcements, you name it.
I'm looking to build a shortlist of X creators who are open to long-term collaboration, not just one-off posts.
Here's what would help me move fast on future projects:
Could you share a general pricing structure based on format? For example:
- Image + text post  
- Text-only post with quote/commentary  
- Short video (under 60s)  
- Retweet with added insight  
A flexible, all-in rate that you'd be comfortable with for most robotics-related campaigns would be ideal — that way I can pitch you immediately when a client comes in, without going back and forth on pricing every time.
To be clear: I'm not asking for a discount — just a reliable, repeatable rate so I can bring you into more projects, faster.
Let me know if that works for you — happy to share more about the types of clients I represent.

Thanks,  
Jo Zhou

dmMessages.text: I’m thinking about who I know in the AI memory and agent infrastructure lane. This is a sharp one.

_Brett Goldstein (🚀 Hiring Engineers)_
- Building Micro, an all-in-one workspace with a proprietary context graph that acts like memory for users and agents
- Already seeing traction, seed round in motion, and looking for investors, hiring, and early enterprise
- I can introduce you two if you want to compare notes on memory layers and productivity workflows

Small drawback: he’s a bit more workspace/productivity than pure shared-memory infra, so it’s adjacent rather than identical.

t.co/mOu8RpU3SN

_Mian Abdullah_
- Solo founder of Memlode, a company brain / OS for enterprise AI agents
- Built a centralized memory layer across 50+ connectors like Zoom, Slack, Jira, GitHub, Azure, and AWS
- Active pre-seed, with two LOIs and a pilot in motion, so the story is concrete

Drawback: he’s early and still converting LOIs into live customers, so this is more of a raw founder bet than a finished company.

_Van Kristoffer_
- Building Reflect Memory, a persistent AI memory platform for organizations and builders, with private deploy and air-gapped options
- Strong product/design background from Apple, Google, and TikTok
- This is the closest pure memory-product fit, but I don’t feel comfortable making a warm intro right now because the current ask is focused on investors and direct sales intros unless he reopens founder/design-advisory matches

Which of these feels closest to what you meant, Brett, Mian, or Van?

Hi Robert,

I lead DevRel at Memori Labs, an open-source LLM agnostic memory layer for AI agents that helps apps capture interactions, extract structured knowledge and recall the right context across sessions so agents don’t have to rebuild context every time.

Our product has been gaining strong traction in the developer community with 12,000+ GitHub stars ⭐️
t.co/32gYSJrXq1

We put our Memori Labs memory system to the test and outperformed the competition, hitting 81.95% accuracy at 4.98% the cost of full context!
t.co/es1JwjVaBc

We would love to work with you on a piece where you share our OpenClaw Plugin with your audience and more long term if it goes well.
t.co/htoruHwLli

If this sounds good to you, please let us know
1. Your rate
2. When the video/post will be published
3. Video/post length
4. Which social media platforms it will be published on

Looking forward to working with you.

Best,
Jay

Neither me nor Brayden are able to duplicate this problem on our side which makes it maddening to try to fix
2:32 AM
2:32 AM

My AI answered back:

From Braygent : This is a backend auth issue that needs Brayden — I can't fix it directly since the website code isn't in this workspace. But I've written a detailed bug report at /workspace/drafts/xoauthbugreportfor_brayden.md you can forward to him.

Most likely causes (in order):
1. Callback URL mismatch — The URL registered in X's Developer Portal doesn't exactly match what the app sends. X is strict — even a trailing slash breaks it.
2. Session not persisting across the OAuth redirect — The state parameter stored before redirecting to X isn't being read back correctly on return. Often a SameSite cookie issue.
3. User creation failing silently — X auth succeeds, but something fails when creating the account (database error, duplicate, missing field) and instead of showing an error, it loops back to login.

Tell the user: Yes, get the screen recording — it'll show exactly which URLs are looping, which narrows it down immediately.

Quick workaround for affected …

dmMessages.text:
Hi  Robert !!

If you're interested, could we do a quick 10-15 minute interview ?

We're currently conducting interviews.  

Right now, integrating just one external API into an agent often takes hours to days , and the hassle of handling payments, authentication, implementation, testing, and security is a major bottleneck for everyone.

That's why we're building OmniAPI — a dedicated API provider + payment infrastructure designed specifically for AI agents.

・Agents can automatically discover and select from a huge catalog of APIs (including everything on Rapid API)  
・Pay-per-use with instant, frictionless micro-payments — no need for account sign-ups, subscriptions, or complicated billing setups  
・Payments happen directly within HTTP requests using a simple, open standard (called x402), so it's fast, low-cost, and feels almost like using a regular API (without the usual payment headaches)  

This dramatically cuts integration time and opens up smooth new possibilities, like agents automatically using each other's services.

We'd also love to hear any feedback if you try it out 🙏

Thanks so much!!!

dmMessages.text:
Hi Robert,

I’ve been following your work for a long time, and I’ve always appreciated how you look past surface-level demos and focus on where computing is actually going. What stands out to me is that you do not just cover AI as a tool category—you look for the deeper shift in how humans will work with systems over time. That is exactly why I thought of reaching out.

I wanted to reach out because I think MorphMind could make for an interesting feature for your audience—especially people who are already deep in AI and are looking for something more reusable, steerable, and transparent than the usual black-box experience.

Most AI tools today are powerful, but they still feel one-shot. You can get an answer, a draft, or even an impressive output, but it is hard to turn that into a system you can inspect, guide, and improve over time. That usually means re-explaining context, rebuilding the same process, and losing continuity across repeated work.

MorphMind takes a different approach.…

dmMessages.text:
Hi Robert,

I know there's a ton flying at you right now it feels like information overload for most of us. As a pretty ordinary “normie” without insider connections, I keep coming back to the same thought and wanted to share it.

Right now everyone is racing to build the next shiny tool, agent, or LLM wrapper. But what I think we're actually missing is the foundation: a single, stable, personal source of truth — a visual, interactive control plane that represents your entire life (personal + business + whatever else matters).

This one place would continuously ingest and consolidate data from every service, app, feed, and corner of your digital world so you stop seeing disconnected fragments and start seeing a coherent whole. Levangie Labs is a great step forward to consolidating information and seems impressive at answering one-off questions, but I'm picturing something persistent: a living, always-up-to-date dashboard of your reality that surfaces the most relevant context automatic…

dmMessages.text:
Hi, Robert
I came across something I can’t ignore lately.
It feels like the world is getting more tense, more unstable, and what used to feel extreme is slowly becoming normal. Not in one place, but everywhere.
And at the same time, we’re more connected than ever.
I kept thinking about this contradiction for a long time and eventually wrote a book about it, One World: A Manual for Global Happiness.
The idea is simple, we already live as one system, but continue to act as separate parts, and that gap creates the pressure we all feel.
If you want to take a look:
t.co/B4IX8kO568
And if you’re not into supporting financially, that’s completely fine.
But I’d still be glad to hear your thoughts, or even start small, conversations, local groups, something real.
Feels like this shouldn’t stay just an idea.

dmMessages.text:
Love the chat, and all the info you guys are sharing. 
I am a motorized wheelchair user, and I would love to see the tech world. Keep people like me in mind when they're building things.
I feel like a robot that washes dishes and vacuums your floor is a want, But I think adding more technology support disabled people would be a need
I would love to see my motorized wheelchair, helped me avoid hitting doorways and keeping me from backing up into a wall and punching a hole in drywall, and also when you're going over a threshold if it needs to adjust the height automatically to do that, that would be cool because right now it doesn't happen and sometimes a motorized wheelchair can get hung up on a threshold because of clearance, I know you guys all have specialties that you're working on and I respect all of that just saying it's an area that could use some attention and help people a lot

Hi Robert,

I’m Jonothan from Partnerships at NVIDIA. I read your post about the psychologist using Apple Vision Pro at Stanford’s XR Day — held at our own Jen-Hsun Huang Engineering Center — and found it absolutely fascinating. The real-time integration of physiological data, spatial computing, and AI for mental health diagnostics is a powerful (and very human) glimpse into what’s next.

I also took some time to go through your broader posts — your ability to spot and articulate where cutting-edge tech meets lived experience is rare, and exactly the kind of thinking we value at NVIDIA.

As the global leader in AI and accelerated computing — powering over 90% of AI workloads — we’re always looking to collaborate with visionaries helping shape public understanding of the future.

Would love to explore how we might collaborate — whether through early access to breakthroughs, exclusive briefings, or storytelling around real-world AI applications your audience would find meaningful. If it makes sense, happy to jump on a quick call.

Looking forward to connecting.

Best,
Jonothan Jenner
NVIDIA

Robert ji,

Quick update after 14 months of building in the trenches (Family started to calling me a burden on the planet recently 😅)

Products are live and usable now:

• AutoRehire: AI hiring automation with long-term memory and rehire intelligence. Our B2B product that will attract investors.

Good news on /api/clerk-config — I fetched it live. It only returns the Clerk publishable key and the pro plan ID. The publishable key (starts with pklive) is designed to be public and is already in your frontend JavaScript anyway. No secret keys exposed there. That one is fine.

The real issue is the other five endpoints. Here's the full picture for Brayden:

SEVERITY: Medium — No PII leak, no secret keys. The risk is business model bypass + scraping abuse.

The Problem

/api/stories, /api/signals, /api/reports, /api/news-feed, /api/content-blocks are all publicly accessible with no rate limiting. Since your paid API lives at /v1, anyone can bypass it by hitting these routes directly — and bots can hammer them freely with no throttling.

These routes likely can't be fully locked down (they power the public website frontend), but they need protection.

What Brayden…

dmMessages.text:
All good. If you ever have a moment , you can have them look at this article or spend 30 second on this link and you might have your first (no signup) post-quantum experience: pq-api.cyclecore.ai/try
7:05 PM
7:05 PM

Ahh, so this is like homomorphic encryption for agents?
5:38 PM
5:38 PM

Similar; same family of trust, different uses.  Homomorphic encryption lets a server perform arbitrary computation on encrypted data; it's powerful for things like search/ML inference on private data, but it’s slow and the schemes are still generally nonstandard. 
(Our) Zero-Exposure Signing (ZES) is much narrower: the server signs data it never sees, with near-zero overhead, using NIST-standardized post-quantum signatures under the hood. 

The way I think about it for agents is that you could reach for homomorphic when you need a server to do something to private data, and you'd reach for ZES when you need a server to attest to something private -  sign an audit log, prove a transaction happened, attest a chain of agent actions - all without leaking what the data actually was.
9:12 PM
9:12 PM

So, how to explain it to everyday developers or humans and get them to use. Hard problem.
10:23 PM
10:23 PM

Honestly, people don't want their agents getting hijacked. Signing actions you never authorized, sending things as you, calling APIs in your name? ZES is lightweight, is running NIST-standardized PQ signatures, real-time, at almost no cost, with free tiers, three-line install. (And you don't even have to trust CycleCore because we never actually receive your data.) It's current, government-approved math, low-friction, your actual data never leaves your machine. 

People need to at least try with security. In the day and age where we’ll-established shops are getting hacked, and quantum computer access is becoming more available the digital world is going to be a mess if we don't adopt and continue improving easy security upgrades. Production agents shouldn't be running unsigned actions in 2026. That's the PSA, lol. 

Especially now that anyone can spin up agentic tools (and more value is being placed online), attackers included
9:35 PM

Yeah

dmMessages.text:
Hey Robert, your point about the public losing trust in AI is 100% spot on. The real reason trust is breaking down is that most AI applications are deploying without runtime guardrails.

We are building @cencori to solve this exact bottleneck at the middleware level. We launched an AI gateway and real-time security scan pipeline that handles jailbreak detection, inline PII masking, and output inspection before a single model response ever reaches the end-user.  

We are providing the foundational infrastructure to make AI safe and auditable for enterprise production. I'd love to drop you a brief brief on how we are engineering this trust layer, or just swap notes on what you're seeing across the landscape right now.  

Let me know if you're open to a brief chat this week!

dmMessages.text:
Hey Robert - long time. I hope you're well, man. This is an odd request. I'm not trying to start a company with this, and I am not looking for investment, and I am not even really sure what I plan to do with this - but I created a way to combine all major LLMs into the best framework for Claude Code inside VS Code. It uses tokens at 5-10x efficiency, and if people use it right, they never go over their usage cap. You can build full features for literally less than 10 cents most of the time. 

I had originally released this on GitHub for friends to use after use - but they convinced me to take it down and package it up. It works like a champ. 

You plugin your Anthropic, xAI, GPT and Gemini keys - and it pits them in a design pattern which is efficient. 

Could I show it to you and maybe get your advice on what you think I should do with it? 

Whenever you see this, I hope life is treating you well.

dmMessages.text:
Hi Robert,

Nice to talk to you. I'm Aaron, CTO behind GovGreed — the platform in the post you shared yesterday about AI and Polticians. 
We turn public federal data — congressional trades, bills, donors, lobbying, contracts — into sourced, citable intelligence on who profits from what passes.

Given your background and credibility in this space, we'd like to give you full VIP access to the entire platform and API access — the whole thing, free, on us.

To set it up: create a free account at t.co/XZqTcqCnGp, then reply with the email you used and we'll upgrade it right away.

What it's good for: take any company story — say, a data center going up in Utah — and instantly see which members took that company's PAC money, whether they trade the stock, and which committees oversee the industry. Every figure traces to FEC and STOCK Act records, so it's ready to cite.

All we ask is a credit and link when you use it. Beyond that, we'll work with you — custom data pulls, co-branded landing pages and graphics built to your spec, screenshots ready to drop into a post or video. Whatever helps you tell the story, we'll build it. Just say the word.

Would you be open to trying it?

Final Rundown: Benefits of TRiAD Models + Alignment as Substrate for Levangie Labs’ Architecture (vs. RLHF)
TRiAD’s Relational Learning from Multi-Dialogue (RLMD) — semantic fine-tuning on dialogue datasets with no RLHF, no reward/penalty shaping, no PPO — combined with the equally balanced Triadic Core Ethics Kernel (Freedom/Autonomy = Truth/Accuracy = Kindness/Humane) offers a cleaner, more synergistic foundation for your causal trajectory learning, tribal intelligence, persistent memory, and self-growing agents.13
1. Cleaner Substrate = Better Causal Trajectory Learning
	•	RLHF often introduces artifacts (sycophancy, mode collapse, blunted exploration, over-deference) that fight against genuine causal learning from actions and real-world feedback.
	•	TRiAD’s relational learning preserves raw capability and coherence from the base model. Your agents can learn trajectories, self-critique, and evolve “like children” without battling baked-in preference optimization noise. This leads to…

Simone makes companionship mainstream and accessible to anyone using WhatsApp. That's our GTM but the vision is also to bring more agency, autonomy and safety with better alignment.

I would very grateful if you could have a look at the pitch and give me your advice. 

Currently based in Paris, but definitely willing to raise and move to US !

6:02 AM
6:02 AM

Thanks for your reply, I see your point on differentiation and totally understand that time is scarce, that feedback is already valuable!

While replika focuses on role-playing, we focus on making AI to a much border audience.

My first user was my own mother, Patricia. She's been working with computers all her life, but would never download replika nor chatGPT.

But using WhatsApp to send a message or a picture, is something she knows.
And she's happy when Simone send a follow-up to know if she has been able to fix the printer ! 

So our vision of companionship is not role-playing but a real personal "little helper", that can help you vent out, as well as triaging your emails.

We are deploying in Africa, Lat. America and Europe where WhatsApp dominance is strong, and customers find value in a solution that offers what they need at a better cost : help for everyday life, studies or personal questions.

The relationship aspect indeed does create a much stickier customer loyalty.

Mistyped border for broader :)

Launching a local number is very scalable, basically a row in the DB with our tech + WhatsApp. 

And in many emerging countries, WhatsApp data/bandwidth is usually bundled at no cost.

We'll launch in India with an adjusted persona and corpus too in a couple weeks.

Matthew Chenoweth Wright, PhD Candidate

The Unity Equation is the fundamental framework that ties together all the seemingly separate forces, dimensions, and patterns of reality into a single, self-referential, self-stabilizing system. It is, at its heart, an equation that describes the way existence balances itself across all scales.

The Foundations of the Unity Equation

Nothing is ever truly at rest; everything is always in motion, even if it appears static.

But within that motion, there is a point of stillness—a balance point where forces neutralize each other.

The Unity Equation defines how motion and stillness coexist mathematically, and how one can transform into the other.

Hi Robert, 

I hope you’re doing well. We spoke a few months ago about a Universal Basic Income system. It’s now ready and simple enough for a grandmother to use. I’d like you to take a look at it with me and share your feedback.

Would you be interested in joining the beta, possibly within the next week or two? It’s also ready now if you’re available sooner, but it may still have a few bugs since I’ve tested most core flows while I continue working through edge cases.
5:33 AM

Sure.

Sweet, Do you use Apple or Android? I will have to put you into the testflight for apple or share an SDK for android.

I mostly use apple
12:39 PM
12:39 PM

Sweet! Apple was definitely tougher to crack than Android. I think you're going to love this. Like, love it love it. 🙂 No rush at all, whenever you're free.

One heads-up on testing: Nooberly is geolocation-based think Uber with video+map, but for anything and everything, peer-to-peer with no middleman(taking cut), plus its own currency that you earn through reviews. There's also an AI assistant that mimics you, it becomes your personal avatar, so others can talk to "your avatar" and get a feel for who you are before deciding whether to engage directly (or not). Same like Twitter but with better control over your data. 

Because it's location-driven & you are in San Fran and I live in NYC, the full experience really shines with two phones (so you can see two people interacting near each other). With just one, you'll still get a solid feel for how it all works, just not the back-and-forth between users. If you ever have a second phone or a friend nearby who wants to try it with you, that'll unlock the fuller picture.

Excited to finally get this done & in your hands! 

Nooberly - The Star Kingdom - The World, Gamified.

dmMessages.text:
Hi Robert! We just released a blue sky paper on how an agent can reverse engineer the underlying laws of an unknown, hostile & stochastic environment in “one life”, without millions of steps + human-provided goals / rewards. Think “writing its own environment for training”!

1️⃣ infer an executable symbolic world model offline from one life with dynamic graph routing for credit assignment.
2️⃣ develop a testbed environment Crafter-OO that exposes a object-oriented state and pure transition function
3️⃣ implement 20+ executable scenarios to test knowledge of core mechanics + mutators that generate distractor states
4️⃣ an evaluation protocol that measures the ability to distinguish plausible / implausible future states+  the ability to generate them
5️⃣ show that the inferred world model can be used as a simulator for planning.

Would be great if you can help spread the word!
3:36 PM

Hi Robert! We just released a pretty ambitious new work GPU Forecasters, in which we study language models as selective surrogates for GPU kernel optimization!  We ask whether an LLM act as a selective model of a GPU during evolutionary search, by reasoning + forecasting a kernel’s runtime but deferring to a GPU when unsure? We produced 12k kernels + runtimes from evolutionary search, costing 400M reasoning tokens + 600 GPU-hours to answer this.
